what_worked_well: Requirements document was exceptionally thorough (836 lines) with complete validation matrix, JSON schema, dependency graph, and locked decisions — minimal ambiguity during epic creation

Parallel subagent invocation (requirements-analyst + architect-reviewer) provided complementary analysis: analyst caught estimation and dependency issues, reviewer caught constitutional compliance and ADR timing issues
Both subagents independently identified the same critical issue (ADR-021 timing) from different perspectives — high-confidence finding
De facto validation of ADR-012 progressive disclosure pattern (60+ existing reference files) resolved what appeared to be a CRITICAL blocker without requiring user intervention
Requirements doc included explicit section cross-references (e.g., FR-002 references FR-001 AC5) reducing ambiguity during feature decomposition
areas_for_improvement: The /create-epic command's argument validation accepts file paths as 'epic names' (76 chars passes the 10-100 length check) — the command should distinguish between epic name strings and requirements file paths

Requirements document dependency graph (Section 10.2) placed ADR-021 after implementation stories, which both reviewers flagged as unconstitutional — requirements elicitation should include a 'constitutional compliance pre-check' step
ADR-012 status discrepancy (Proposed vs de facto standard) indicates ADR lifecycle management gap — 20+ agents use the pattern but the authorizing ADR is still 'Proposed'
Phase 6.6 validation checklist expects priority/business_value frontmatter fields that the actual project convention (EPIC-071 pattern) does not use — template and checklist are misaligned

recommendations.description: Add constitutional compliance pre-check to requirements elicitation workflow — verify dependency graphs respect immutability rules (ADR before structural changes) during /ideate or /create-epic, not after
recommendations.implementation_notes: Add a Phase N.5 step that checks if any proposed new files require source-tree.md authorization and flags ADR creation as Day 0 prerequisite

recommendations.description: Accept ADR-012 formally — it is de facto standard with 60+ reference files across 20+ agents but still 'Proposed' status
recommendations.implementation_notes: Update status from 'Proposed' to 'Accepted', add implementation evidence section citing agent count

recommendations.description: Align epic validation checklist frontmatter expectations with actual project convention — the checklist expects priority/business_value but EPIC-071+ uses start_date/target_date/total_points
recommendations.implementation_notes: Update Section 1 YAML template to match EPIC-071 convention

patterns_observed: Parallel subagent analysis (requirements-analyst + architect-reviewer) consistently catches more issues than sequential analysis — the cross-validation effect is valuable
Requirements documents with explicit dependency graphs accelerate epic creation — the CLAP doc's Section 10.2 graph was directly usable
When requirements include locked decisions (Section 9.3), epic creation AskUserQuestion prompts are minimal (only 1 question needed)
anti_patterns_detected: ADR lifecycle gap: pattern adopted de facto but authorizing ADR never accepted — creates constitutional ambiguity
ai_analysis.constraint_analysis: All CLAP deliverables use approved technologies (Markdown, JSON, haiku model, Read/Glob/Grep tools). No tech-stack.md violations. The alignment-auditor/references/ subdirectory follows established convention despite ADR-012 Proposed status. source-tree.md updates authorized via ADR-021 (moved to Day 0 prerequisite per architect-reviewer recommendation).
